cards.back: A half subtractor is a digital circuit that subtracts two bits from each other, resulting in a sum and a carry-out.
A full subtractor is a digital circuit that subtracts three bits (A, B, and D) from each other, resulting in a sum, a borrow-out, and a carry-out.

cards.front: What are the inputs of a Half Subtractor
cards.back: The inputs of a half subtractor are Bit A, Bit B, and the carry-in (CIN).

cards.front: What are the outputs of a Half Subtractor
cards.back: The outputs of a half subtractor are the sum (S) and the carry-out (COUT).

cards.front: What is the function of the Full Subtractor
cards.back: The full subtractor is used to perform subtraction of three bits, which includes borrowing from the bit above.
